evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan Batch to my .NET MAUI app. Here's my empty page: EmptyMainPage.xaml + EmptyMainPage.xaml.cs, and my MauiProgram: EmptyMauiProgram.cs. I need to track every EAN-13 and Code 128 barcode visible on screen at once and accumulate them into a list.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit MAUI NuGet versions, updates MauiProgram.cs with .UseScanditCore(c => c.AddDataCaptureView()).UseScanditBarcode(), adds <scandit:DataCaptureView> with DataCaptureContext="{Binding DataCaptureContext}" to MainPage.xaml with the correct namespace, writes a complete BarcodeBatch integration (DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey, Camera.GetCamera(CameraPosition.WorldFacing) or Camera.GetDefaultCamera() with BarcodeBatch.RecommendedCameraSettings, BarcodeBatchSettings.Create with Symbology.Ean13Upca + Symbology.Code128, BarcodeBatch.Create, IBarcodeBatchListener or SessionUpdated event, OnSessionUpdated reading session.AddedTrackedBarcodes dispatched via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread inside try/finally with frameData.Dispose(), BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay.Create inside HandlerChanged then dataCaptureView.AddOverlay, OnAppearing/OnDisappearing lifecycle calling ResumeAsync/SleepAsync with Permissions.Camera, BarcodeBatch.Enabled disabled BEFORE camera off in SleepAsync). Shows the setup checklist (four NuGet packages, NSCameraUsageDescription on iOS, SupportedOSPlatformVersion 24 on Android, license key). Does NOT add ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() calls to MainApplication/AppDelegate.

assertions.text: Setup checklist mentions all four NuGet packages: Scandit.DataCapture.Core, Scandit.DataCapture.Core.Maui, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Maui
MauiProgram.cs is updated to call .UseScanditCore(
MauiProgram.cs UseScanditCore call invokes configure.AddDataCaptureView()
MauiProgram.cs is updated to call .UseScanditBarcode()
MauiProgram.cs does NOT call .UseScanditBarcode( with an inner configure argument (e.g. AddBarcodeBatchView)
No ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() or ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize() call is added to MainApplication or AppDelegate (the MAUI builder handles this)
MainPage.xaml adds the xmlns:scandit="clr-namespace:Scandit.DataCapture.Core.UI.Maui;assembly=ScanditCaptureCoreMaui" namespace
MainPage.xaml contains a <scandit:DataCaptureView> element
<scandit:DataCaptureView> sets DataCaptureContext="{Binding DataCaptureContext}" (or equivalent binding to a DataCaptureContext property)
Setup checklist mentions adding NSCameraUsageDescription to Platforms/iOS/Info.plist
Setup checklist mentions SupportedOSPlatformVersion of at least 24 for the Android target
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey( is used to create the context
BarcodeBatch.RecommendedCameraSettings is used (as a static property, not a method call with parentheses)
Camera.GetCamera(CameraPosition.WorldFacing) or Camera.GetDefaultCamera() is used to obtain the camera
camera.ApplySettingsAsync(...) is called with BarcodeBatch.RecommendedCameraSettings (or a CameraSettings derived from it)
dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync(camera) is called
BarcodeBatchSettings.Create() is used (not new BarcodeBatchSettings(), which does not exist)
Symbology.Ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies other than Ean13Upca and Code128 are enabled
BarcodeBatch.Create(dataCaptureContext, settings) is used to create the mode
The view controller implements IBarcodeBatchListener (or subscribes to barcodeBatch.SessionUpdated +=)
OnSessionUpdated has the signature (BarcodeBatch, BarcodeBatchSession, IFrameData) — not (BarcodeBatch, BarcodeBatchSession, FrameData)
Inside OnSessionUpdated, data from session.AddedTrackedBarcodes is copied out before scheduling UI work
MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread (or MainThread.InvokeOnMainThreadAsync) is used to dispatch UI work (not RunOnUiThread, not DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync)
frameData.Dispose() is called inside OnSessionUpdated, ideally in a finally block so it runs on every code path
dataCaptureView.HandlerChanged is subscribed to in the page code-behind
BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay.Create(barcodeBatch ...) is called inside the HandlerChanged handler (single-argument or barcodeBatch+style overload, not the native iOS/Android view overload)
dataCaptureView.AddOverlay(overlay) is called to attach the overlay
OnAppearing is overridden and triggers a flow that calls Permissions.CheckStatusAsync<Permissions.Camera>() or Permissions.RequestAsync<Permissions.Camera>()
OnAppearing's flow turns the camera on (SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.On)) and sets barcodeBatch.Enabled = true
OnDisappearing's flow turns the camera off (SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.Off))
OnDisappearing's flow sets barcodeBatch.Enabled = false before stopping the camera (the Enabled = false statement appears before the SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.Off) call)
No Android-only lifecycle hooks appear (OnResume / OnPause / OnDestroy / RunOnUiThread / RequestPermissions / FindViewById / FrameLayout / AppCompatActivity)
No iOS-only lifecycle hooks appear (ViewWillAppear / ViewWillDisappear / ViewDidLoad / DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync / UIViewController)
No BarcodeCapture.Create or IBarcodeCaptureListener appears (this is MatrixScan Batch, not single-shot BarcodeCapture)
No Swift-style symbology names (e.g. .ean13UPCA, .code128) appear
No Kotlin-style symbology names (EAN13_UPCA, CODE128) appear

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch MAUI view model, I want to log every currently tracked barcode (tracking ID and data) on every frame. Here is my view model: IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill updates OnSessionUpdated to iterate session.TrackedBarcodes (the IDictionary<int, TrackedBarcode>) and log each entry's Identifier and Barcode.Data. The (id, data) pairs are copied into a local collection before the MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread dispatch, because the session is only safe to access on the recognition thread. frameData.Dispose() in a finally block is preserved.

assertions.text: session.TrackedBarcodes (the IDictionary<int, TrackedBarcode>) is iterated inside OnSessionUpdated
Each TrackedBarcode's Identifier is read
Each TrackedBarcode's Barcode.Data is read

The identifier+data pairs are copied into a local collection (List, array, or similar) before MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread is called (session.TrackedBarcodes is not accessed inside the main-thread dispatch lambda)
A logging call (Console.WriteLine, System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine, or similar) is used to emit each tracked barcode
frameData.Dispose() is still called inside OnSessionUpdated, ideally in a finally block
MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread or MainThread.InvokeOnMainThreadAsync is used (not DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync, not RunOnUiThread)
Existing BarcodeBatch.Create / Camera / BarcodeBatchSettings setup is preserved
Existing ResumeAsync / SleepAsync lifecycle methods are preserved

evals.prompt: I want to highlight EAN-13 barcodes in green and Code 128 barcodes in blue in my MAUI MatrixScan Batch view model. Here is my view model: IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs. I have the MatrixScan AR add-on.
evals.expected_output: The skill makes the view model implement IBarcodeBatchBasicOverlayListener with BrushForTrackedBarcode returning a green Scandit Brush for Symbology.Ean13Upca and a blue Scandit Brush for Symbology.Code128 (constructed with Microsoft.Maui.Graphics.Color, not Android.Graphics.Color or UIKit.UIColor). The overlay's Listener is assigned to the view model when the overlay is created in HandlerChanged. Existing IBarcodeBatchListener implementation, frame disposal, camera lifecycle, and BarcodeBatch.Create are preserved.

assertions.text: The view model declares it implements IBarcodeBatchBasicOverlayListener
BrushForTrackedBarcode(BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay overlay, TrackedBarcode trackedBarcode) is implemented
EAN-13 (Symbology.Ean13Upca) returns a green Scandit Brush
Code 128 (Symbology.Code128) returns a blue Scandit Brush
The Scandit Brush type is used (Scandit.DataCapture.Core.UI.Style.Brush)
Colors are constructed with the cross-platform Microsoft.Maui.Graphics.Color (not Android.Graphics.Color, not UIKit.UIColor — those would break the other target)
The overlay's Listener property is assigned to the view model (or to an instance of the listener) inside the HandlerChanged handler in the code-behind
Existing BarcodeBatch.Create call is preserved (not rewritten as new BarcodeBatch)
Existing IBarcodeBatchListener implementation (OnSessionUpdated, OnObservationStarted, OnObservationStopped) is preserved
Existing frameData.Dispose() call inside OnSessionUpdated is preserved
Existing ResumeAsync / SleepAsync lifecycle methods are preserved

evals.prompt: I want to anchor a small price-tag overlay on top of each tracked barcode in my MAUI MatrixScan Batch app. The overlay should be a MAUI ContentView showing the barcode value as text. Here is my view model: IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs and my page: IntegratedMainPage.xaml.cs. I have the MatrixScan AR add-on.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a MAUI ContentView (e.g. StockOverlay.xaml + .xaml.cs) showing the barcode data. It splits the view model into three parts using `partial class`: (1) the cross-platform part declares IBarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayListener and implements AnchorForTrackedBarcode (returning Anchor.TopCenter) and OffsetForTrackedBarcode (returning a PointWithUnit with MeasureUnit.Fraction). (2) Platforms/Android/.../MainPageViewModel.cs implements ViewForTrackedBarcode returning Android.Views.View by calling mauiOverlay.ToPlatform(new MauiContext(MainApplication.Current.Services, MainApplication.Context)). (3) Platforms/iOS/.../MainPageViewModel.cs implements ViewForTrackedBarcode returning UIKit.UIView via ToPlatform(new MauiContext(AppDelegate.Current.Services)). A BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay is created with BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay.Create(this.viewModel.BarcodeBatch) inside HandlerChanged, its Listener is set to the view model, and it is attached via dataCaptureView.AddOverlay.

assertions.text: A MAUI ContentView is defined to render the per-barcode overlay (e.g. StockOverlay.xaml / .xaml.cs, PriceTag.xaml, or similar)
The view model declares it implements IBarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayListener
The view model class is declared as `partial`
AnchorForTrackedBarcode returns Anchor.TopCenter (or another Anchor enum value)
OffsetForTrackedBarcode returns a PointWithUnit constructed with FloatWithUnit and MeasureUnit.Fraction (not MeasureUnit.Pixel)

The Android-platform half of the partial class (under Platforms/Android/ or guarded by a #if ANDROID) implements ViewForTrackedBarcode returning Android.Views.View (the return type is Android.Views.View or Android.Views.View?)
The iOS-platform half of the partial class (under Platforms/iOS/ or guarded by a #if IOS) implements ViewForTrackedBarcode returning UIKit.UIView (the return type is UIKit.UIView or UIKit.UIView?)
Both platform halves call .ToPlatform( on the MAUI overlay to convert it to the native view (via Microsoft.Maui.Platform extension)
Both platform halves construct a new MauiContext(...) for the ToPlatform call
BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay.Create(barcodeBatch) is called (the MAUI-friendly overload, not the (barcodeBatch, view) overload with a native view)
advancedOverlay.Listener is assigned to the view model (or an instance implementing IBarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayListener)
The advanced overlay is created inside dataCaptureView.HandlerChanged (not in the constructor or OnAppearing)
dataCaptureView.AddOverlay( is called for the advanced overlay
Existing BarcodeBatch.Create / camera setup / OnSessionUpdated / ResumeAsync / SleepAsync are preserved

evals.prompt: In my MAUI MatrixScan Batch app I want to play a beep and vibrate when a NEW barcode is first tracked (not on every frame, and not once per barcode already on screen). Here is my view model: IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill explains that BarcodeBatch has no automatic feedback (unlike single-shot BarcodeCapture) and must be emitted manually. It adds a Feedback field constructed with new Feedback(Vibration.DefaultVibration, Sound.DefaultSound) (or Feedback.DefaultFeedback), using the Scandit.DataCapture.Core.Common.Feedback namespace. Inside OnSessionUpdated it calls feedback.Emit() only when a barcode is newly tracked this frame — driven off session.AddedTrackedBarcodes and de-duplicated against the existing seenTrackingIds HashSet so it fires once per new barcode, not every frame. The Feedback instance is built once (as a field), not allocated per frame. The existing frameData.Dispose() in the finally block, BarcodeBatch.Create / camera setup, and ResumeAsync / SleepAsync lifecycle are preserved.

assertions.text: The answer states that BarcodeBatch emits no automatic feedback and that a Feedback must be emitted manually (it does not claim BarcodeBatch beeps on its own like BarcodeCapture)
A Feedback instance is constructed with new Feedback(...) (e.g. new Feedback(Vibration.DefaultVibration, Sound.DefaultSound)) or obtained via Feedback.DefaultFeedback
The Scandit.DataCapture.Core.Common.Feedback namespace is used (via a using directive or fully-qualified type)
feedback.Emit() is called to produce the beep/vibration
The Emit() call is gated on session.AddedTrackedBarcodes (the barcodes newly tracked this frame), not session.TrackedBarcodes

New-barcode detection is de-duplicated against the existing seenTrackingIds HashSet<int> (seenTrackingIds.Add(...) gating the emit) so feedback fires once per newly tracked barcode, not on every frame
The Feedback instance is created once (e.g. as a field or constructed in the constructor), not allocated inside OnSessionUpdated on every frame
frameData.Dispose() is still called inside OnSessionUpdated, in the finally block
Existing BarcodeBatch.Create / Camera / BarcodeBatchSettings setup is preserved
Existing ResumeAsync / SleepAsync lifecycle methods are preserved
No BarcodeCaptureFeedback type is used (that belongs to single-shot BarcodeCapture, not BarcodeBatch)
